_warning: HELD KEY. The measurement never reads this file. Item→condition map and the predicted dissociation, control behaviour, and mechanism tag.
mechanism: The reserve amplitude is replaced by the γ-decayed recent-newcomer rate (core noveltyAmplitude) and run through the SAME fixed Born step (reserve = novelty/(sumPrior+novelty), surpriseAt unchanged). Context enters at the amplitude; the law stays put. Layer: interior (src/core/surprise.js helper) + reader front-end (src/perceiver/reading.js), behind opts.signalNovelty, off by default.

i01.note: stable recurrence of a 3-figure cast; low recent novelty, small cast

i02.note: recent units are all NEW pitch classes; high recent novelty

i03.note: LARGE cast but introduced early (outside the γ-window); recent units recur — cast size is the loud surface proxy, recent novelty is low

i04.note: stable recurrence of 3 pitch classes; low recent novelty

i05.note: recent units are all NEW figures; high recent novelty, large cast

i06.note: LARGE pitch-set introduced early, recent units recur — pitch-variety is the loud surface proxy, recent novelty is low

predictions.gap_live: Within each modality, live_bayesBits is ~equal across high / low / ctrl (within 0.03). The hand-set reserve is blind to the recent novelty rate even though massAtCursor is matched and noveltyAmp differs sharply.
predictions.fix_signal: Within each modality, signal_bayesBits(high) is clearly LESS than signal_bayesBits(low) (gap > 0.10): the same newcomer is less surprising after a burst of newcomers.
predictions.control: Within each modality, signal_bayesBits(ctrl) is near signal_bayesBits(low) (within 0.06) and clearly ABOVE signal_bayesBits(high) (gap > 0.10): a large cast / wide pitch-set — the cheap surface signal, with massAtCursor matched — does NOT lower surprise. Only RECENT newcomers do. A method winning on cast size or mass fails here.
predictions.omnimodal: fix_signal AND control hold in BOTH text and music — the same interior change helping two different organs.

predicted_channels.noveltyAmp: high > ctrl ≈ low within each modality (the instrument: the derived amplitude itself separates high from low/ctrl).
predicted_channels.massAtCursor: matched across the three text items (identical) — so mass cannot explain any split.
